g.title: LA real person who became the cultural archetype/exemplar of a legendary identity. Declared as a new term: no existing property in CIDOC-CRM, schema.org, SKOS or EAC-CPF models a person-to-concept exemplification relation without a forced domain/range mismatch.|Language: en
g.text: A real person who became the cultural archetype/exemplar of a legendary identity. Declared as a new term: no existing property in CIDOC-CRM, schema.org, SKOS or EAC-CPF models a person-to-concept exemplification relation without a forced domain/range mismatch.

g.title: Rninja:embodiesArchetype->LA real person who became the cultural archetype/exemplar of a legendary identity. Declared as a new term: no existing property in CIDOC-CRM, schema.org, SKOS or EAC-CPF models a person-to-concept exemplification relation without a forced domain/range mismatch.|Language: en

LA feudal-era person's ongoing service obligation to their lord. No CIDOC-CRM or schema.org property models this without reifying it as its own Activity/Employment event; EAC-CPF's cpfRelation is the closest existing model (built for person/family/corporate-body relations) but its own relation-type vocabulary has no term this specific, so this narrower property is declared as its sub-property.|Language: en
g.text: A feudal-era person's ongoing service obligation to their lord. No CIDOC-CRM or schema.org property models this without reifying it as its own Activity/Employment event; EAC-CPF's cpfRelation is the closest existing model (built for person/family/corporate-body relations) but its own relation-type vocabulary has no term this specific, so this narrower property is declared as its sub-property.

g.title: Rninja:isVassalOf->LA feudal-era person's ongoing service obligation to their lord. No CIDOC-CRM or schema.org property models this without reifying it as its own Activity/Employment event; EAC-CPF's cpfRelation is the closest existing model (built for person/family/corporate-body relations) but its own relation-type vocabulary has no term this specific, so this narrower property is declared as its sub-property.|Language: en

LDefined more by skillset and function than by social rank or class; the identity could be adopted situationally rather than marking a fixed separate caste, and historical samurai are recorded taking on ninja roles.
g.text: Defined more by skillset and function than by social rank or class; the identity could be adopted situationally rather than marking a fixed separate caste, and historical samurai are recorded taking on ninja roles.

g.title: Rwd:Q9402->LDefined more by skillset and function than by social rank or class; the identity could be adopted situationally rather than marking a fixed separate caste, and historical samurai are recorded taking on ninja roles.
LLocated near Iga Ueno Castle; includes a model ninja village open to tours.
g.text: Located near Iga Ueno Castle; includes a model ninja village open to tours.

g.title: Rwd:Q4306401->LLocated near Iga Ueno Castle; includes a model ninja village open to tours.
LOriginally a stabbing/thrusting tool rather than a throwing weapon; the "thrown ninja knife" image comes from modern fiction.
g.text: Originally a stabbing/thrusting tool rather than a throwing weapon; the "thrown ninja knife" image comes from modern fiction.

g.title: Rwd:Q860340->LOriginally a stabbing/thrusting tool rather than a throwing weapon; the "thrown ninja knife" image comes from modern fiction.
LDivided into 4 districts (郡) with about 197 villages before its abolition.
g.text: Divided into 4 districts (郡) with about 197 villages before its abolition.

g.title: Rwd:Q1047128->LDivided into 4 districts (郡) with about 197 villages before its abolition.
LDespite depictions as shoes for walking on water, the design more plausibly centred on a wooden platform meant to be sat on rather than stood on, functioning as a buoyancy aid rather than footwear.
g.text: Despite depictions as shoes for walking on water, the design more plausibly centred on a wooden platform meant to be sat on rather than stood on, functioning as a buoyancy aid rather than footwear.

g.title: Rwd:Q6884757->LDespite depictions as shoes for walking on water, the design more plausibly centred on a wooden platform meant to be sat on rather than stood on, functioning as a buoyancy aid rather than footwear.
LPer the Bansenshūkai ninja of the Fujibayashi family reportedly used this water-flotation device.
g.text: Per the Bansenshūkai ninja of the Fujibayashi family reportedly used this water-flotation device.

g.title: Rwd:Q6884757->LPer the Bansenshūkai ninja of the Fujibayashi family reportedly used this water-flotation device.

LNot a single unified martial art but a collective term for numerous distinct schools (ryūha), often family-based, whose techniques were historically transmitted primarily through oral instruction rather than a standardized curriculum.
g.text: Not a single unified martial art but a collective term for numerous distinct schools (ryūha), often family-based, whose techniques were historically transmitted primarily through oral instruction rather than a standardized curriculum.

g.title: Rwd:Q539067->LNot a single unified martial art but a collective term for numerous distinct schools (ryūha), often family-based, whose techniques were historically transmitted primarily through oral instruction rather than a standardized curriculum.
LMatsudaira Motoyasu's forces took hostages to trade for his own family then held by the Imagawa clan.
g.text: Matsudaira Motoyasu's forces took hostages to trade for his own family then held by the Imagawa clan.

g.title: Rwd:Q7510141->LMatsudaira Motoyasu's forces took hostages to trade for his own family then held by the Imagawa clan.
LCommanded the shogunate's yoriki officers and 200 civil officials after Ieyasu's move to the Kantō region.
g.text: Commanded the shogunate's yoriki officers and 200 civil officials after Ieyasu's move to the Kantō region.

g.title: Rwd:Q316952->LCommanded the shogunate's yoriki officers and 200 civil officials after Ieyasu's move to the Kantō region.
LRetired as a Buddhist monk and founded Sainen-ji temple in Edo (now Shinjuku Tokyo) where he is buried.
g.text: Retired as a Buddhist monk and founded Sainen-ji temple in Edo (now Shinjuku Tokyo) where he is buried.

g.title: Rwd:Q316952->LRetired as a Buddhist monk and founded Sainen-ji temple in Edo (now Shinjuku Tokyo) where he is buried.

LCompiled in 1676, after the Sengoku-period height of ninja activity had passed and during a long peace under Tokugawa rule; its compiler was not a contemporary of the practices it records but drew on earlier oral and written tradition.
g.text: Compiled in 1676, after the Sengoku-period height of ninja activity had passed and during a long peace under Tokugawa rule; its compiler was not a contemporary of the practices it records but drew on earlier oral and written tradition.

g.title: Rwd:Q806956->LCompiled in 1676, after the Sengoku-period height of ninja activity had passed and during a long peace under Tokugawa rule; its compiler was not a contemporary of the practices it records but drew on earlier oral and written tradition.
LSurvives in two main manuscript traditions: a Kōga version (22 chapters in 10+1 volumes) and an Iga version (22 chapters in 12+4 volumes).
g.text: Survives in two main manuscript traditions: a Kōga version (22 chapters in 10+1 volumes) and an Iga version (22 chapters in 12+4 volumes).

g.title: Rwd:Q806956->LSurvives in two main manuscript traditions: a Kōga version (22 chapters in 10+1 volumes) and an Iga version (22 chapters in 12+4 volumes).
LTranslated into English as "The Book of Ninja" by Antony Cummins and Yoshie Minami (2013).

LHistorically associated above all with the Hattori clan and the Fujibayashi clan.
